title: Position: Breaking the Dual Curse of Multilingual AI Requires Socio-Technical Guardrails, Not Post-Hoc Alignment
venueLong: 43rd International Conference on Machine Learning (ICML '26), Position Paper Track, Seoul, South Korea
abstract: Multilingual language models exhibit a dual curse — harmful generation rises to 35% in low-resource languages while instruction-following collapses. Reward models achieve only 49–50% accuracy in low-resource languages, undermining post-deployment safety. We argue for socio-technical guardrails built in from pre-training.

title: DIA-HARM: Dialectal Disparities in Harmful Content Detection Across 50 English Dialects
venueLong: 64th Annual Meeting of the Association for Computational Linguistics (ACL '26), San Diego, CA, USA
abstract: The first benchmark for evaluating disinformation detection robustness across 50 English dialects. Introduces the D3 corpus (195K samples) and evaluates 16 detection models, revealing systematic disparities across dialect groups.
